hero.title: How to Create a Professional DevOps Resume
hero.subtitle: Resume strategies that help students pass ATS systems and get interviews.

structure.title: Summary
structure.desc: A brief 2-3 sentence overview of your experience, highlighting key cloud and automation skills.

structure.title: Skills
structure.desc: Categorize by Cloud, CI/CD, IaC, Containers, and Scripting.

structure.title: Experience
structure.desc: Focus on impact, numbers, and the technologies used.

structure.title: Projects
structure.desc: Detail real-world projects showing end-to-end implementation.

structure.title: Certifications
structure.desc: List relevant certs like AWS Solutions Architect or CKA.

structure.title: Education
structure.desc: Keep it brief, placing it after experience if you have relevant work history.

resumeExample.name: Jane Doe
resumeExample.title: Cloud / DevOps Engineer
resumeExample.summary: Results-driven DevOps Engineer with expertise in AWS, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. Proven ability to automate infrastructure and reduce deployment times by 40%.

skills.Cloud: AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, EKS, IAM, VPC)

bullets: Automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform, reducing setup time by 50%.
Implemented CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions for containerized applications.
Monitored cluster health using Prometheus and Grafana.

tips.title: ATS Optimization
tips.desc: Use standard section headings and avoid complex formatting or tables.

tips.title: Keywords
tips.desc: Include tools directly mentioned in the job description (e.g., 'Kubernetes', 'Terraform').

tips.title: Metrics and achievements
tips.desc: Use the XYZ formula: Accomplished [X] as measured by [Y], by doing [Z].

tips.title: Common mistakes
tips.desc: Avoid rating your skills (e.g., 4/5 Python) and spelling errors on tool names.

tips.title: Real recruiter expectations
tips.desc: Recruiters want to see what you actually *built*, not just a list of buzzwords.
